sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CRDF - Trovagene Presents Outlook for Second Half of 2018
feed.summary: Trovagene (NASDAQ: TROV), an oncology therapeutics company, provided an outlook for the second half of 2018, emphasizing progress in two key clinical trials for PCM-075 targeting Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) and metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC). The company highlighted significant achievements in the first half of 2018, including encouraging preliminary clinical data and progress in trial enrollments. Trovagene aims to complete dose escalation, present safety/efficacy data, and initiate Phase 2 segments of its AML trial, while also evaluating strategic partnerships and searching for a new CEO.

feed.title: Axon Enterprise Inc. Is Quietly Taking Over – But Is This Stock a Hidden Cheat Code or Overhyped C
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise Inc., known for its tasers and body cameras, is gaining significant attention and trading near all-time highs. The company benefits from a unique position at the intersection of tech, policing, and viral content, leveraging its hardware with a strong recurring software revenue model through its Axon Cloud platform. While its valuation is high, investors are drawn to its robust growth, profitability, and essential role in modern public safety.

Investors are eyeing Cencora as a long-term, fundamentals-first play with defensive potential, rather than a high-hype stock.

feed.title: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages Varonis Systems, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - VRNS
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors of Varonis Systems, Inc. who purchased securities between February 4, 2025, and October 28, 2025, about the upcoming March 9, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Varonis made materially false and/or misleading statements regarding its ability to maintain ARR projections while converting customers to SaaS, and its capacity to retain customers on its platform. The firm encourages affected investors to secure counsel and consider joining the class action to potentially recover damages.

feed.title: ROSEN, A RANKED AND LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Ardent Health, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - ARDT
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors of Ardent Health, Inc. (NYSE: ARDT) who purchased securities between July 18, 2024, and November 12, 2025, about the March 9, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Ardent Health made misrepresentations regarding its accounts receivable and professional malpractice liability insurance, leading to investor damages. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm to secure counsel and potentially recover losses.

feed.title: Woman sues HomeGoods in Ocala after tripping over employee working on rug
feed.summary: A Marion County woman has filed a lawsuit against The TJX Companies, Inc., parent company of HomeGoods, after allegedly tripping over an employee working on a rug at a store in Ocala. Kathryn Monaco is seeking over $50,000 in damages for bodily injury and suffering, claiming the company was negligent and created a dangerous condition. The incident occurred on October 3, 2025, at the HomeGoods located at Market Street at Heathbrook.

feed.title: The Truth About Generac Holdings: Backup Power Stock That Everyone Slept On
feed.summary: Generac Holdings is a company leveraging the increasing demand for home backup power due to climate change and grid instability. While not a trendy meme stock, it's a volatile, mid-to-large-cap company with real products and services, positioning itself as a core play in the energy resilience sector. The article examines Generac's market position, competitive landscape, and investment potential for those seeking exposure to solutions for unreliable power grids.

feed.title: C&F Bank taps Navy veteran and VCU professor for board role
feed.summary: C&F Financial Corporation (NASDAQ: CFFI) has announced the appointment of Dr. David H. Downs to its Board of Directors and the board of C&F Bank, effective February 24, 2026. Dr. Downs, an Alfred L. Blake Endowed Chair Professor at Virginia Commonwealth University and former U.S. Navy officer and management consultant, brings expertise in real estate, investments, and risk management. He has served on the C&F Central Virginia Advisory Board for over 12 years.

feed.title: Red Cross gets $100K boost to open Carolinas warming shelters
feed.summary: The Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a $100,000 rapid response grant to the American Red Cross to aid communities in North Carolina and South Carolina ahead of Winter Storm Fern. These funds will support warming shelters, provide emergency supplies, and assist community-run shelter operations as severe cold weather is anticipated. This initiative is part of Duke Energy's broader commitment to customer support and storm readiness, reinforcing community resilience.

feed.title: New Year, New Growth: 3 Stocks Under $2B Breaking Out in 2026
feed.summary: This article highlights three small-cap companies—Kraken Robotics (KRKNF), Enovix (ENVX), and Aehr Test Systems (AEHR)—that are poised for significant growth in 2026 due to critical operational milestones. Kraken Robotics secured major subsea battery orders, Enovix achieved manufacturing scalability for its next-gen batteries, and Aehr Test Systems successfully pivoted to AI processor testing. These developments position them as compelling investment opportunities outside traditional large-cap markets.

feed.title: Eversource Energy: Defensive Utility Or Value Trap? Wall Street Rethinks This New England Powerhouse
feed.summary: Eversource Energy's stock has been performing poorly, nearing its 52-week low, as investors are cautious about regulated utilities facing higher rates, grid upgrade costs, and clean energy uncertainties. Analysts have mixed ratings, ranging from "Hold" to "Buy," signaling limited near-term excitement. The company's future hinges on interest rates, successful grid modernization, and managing its offshore wind exposures.

feed.title: The Coca-Cola Company (KO) Accelerates Consumer-Centric Execution and Enterprise-Wide Digital Transformation With Key Leadership Changes
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company announced key leadership and organizational changes to accelerate consumer-centric execution and digital transformation, including the appointment of Henrique Braun as CEO and creation of a Chief Digital Officer role. These changes aim to speed decision-making and embed technology. Separately, the company is reportedly abandoning plans to sell Costa Coffee due to bids falling short of its target, highlighting ongoing portfolio challenges.

feed.title: Xcel Energy’s Fortune Honor Highlights Social Focus And Grid Investment Story
feed.summary: Xcel Energy (NasdaqGS:XEL) has been recognized for the twelfth year on Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies list, ranking first in social responsibility within its industry. This honor comes as the company continues to invest in grid modernization and resilience, balancing reliability, cost, and decarbonization. While the recognition reinforces Xcel Energy's reputation as a steady operator, investors should also consider risks such as interest payments not well covered by earnings and dividend payments not fully covered by free cash flow.

feed.title: CHS expands into St. Louis to strengthen center gulf supply chain
feed.summary: CHS Inc. is expanding its presence on the Mississippi River by assuming operations of a key grain facility in Cahokia, Illinois, from COFCO International Ltd. This move, effective January 31, 2026, aims to strengthen the cooperative's supply chain, enhance market access for U.S. farmers, and support its center gulf export strategy. The facility offers year-round access to the St. Louis Harbor and direct connections to Class I railroads, optimizing high-speed grain loading.
